title: Agent Smith Configuration
description: Schema for agentsmith.yml (and agentsmith.example.yml). Introduced in p0139: top-level named catalogs for agents, repos, trackers, and pipeline_triggers; projects reference catalog entries by name.

agents.description: Catalog of AI agents. Each key is a name referenced by projects.

connections.description: p0281a: catalog of git-host connections. Repos are DISCOVERED under a connection via the provider API; a project selects them with connection/glob (e.g. 'acme/Sample.App.*', '!acme/Sample.App.Ui.Tests') instead of enumerating each repo.

repos.description: Catalog of source repositories (legacy / escape hatch for a single non-discoverable repo). Prefer connections + connection/glob references.

trackers.description: Catalog of issue/work-item trackers. Each key is a name referenced by projects.

pipeline_triggers.description: Global map of label/tag string to pipeline name. Project-level trigger blocks fall back to this when their pipeline_from_label is omitted.

projects.description: Per-project configuration. Each project references catalog entries by name plus declares its own trigger blocks.

deployment.description: p0281c: single deployment-wide image pin feeding BOTH the orchestrator and the sandbox-agent image (they ship from the same release). The legacy sandbox.agent_version / orchestrator.{registry,version} blocks still load and win when set.

agent.description: AI agent definition. Sub-blocks (retry, cache, compaction, models, pricing, parallelism) are passed through to AgentConfig and validated separately by the loader.

network_timeout_seconds.description: Per-request LLM HTTP timeout (default 300). The SDK default is 100s; a large completion exceeds it and the run dies with 'A task was cancelled'.

repo.description: Source repository connection. type=local uses path; remote types use url.

connection.description: p0281a: a git-host connection. Repos are discovered under it via the provider API. azure_devops needs organization+project; github needs owner; gitlab needs group. host overrides the cloud endpoint for self-hosted installs.

host.description: Optional API host override for self-hosted GitHub Enterprise / GitLab.

default_branch.description: Fallback branch used only until discovery reads each repo's real default branch.

tracker.description: Issue/work-item tracker connection. Open states, done status, close transition, and extra fields belong here when shared across projects.

trigger_statuses.description: p0281b: tracker-owned run-trigger gate inherited by every project routed here; falls back to open_states when unset. A project trigger overrides it.

failed_status.description: p0281b: tracker-owned failed_status base; a project trigger overrides it.

pipeline_from_label.description: p0281b: tracker-owned label->pipeline map; a project trigger overrides it, then the global pipeline_triggers map is the final fallback.

zero_match_comment.description: When true (and the provider supports comments), posts a 'no agent-smith project matched this ticket' note on tickets whose ProjectResolver lookup returns zero matches. Default false. Single-tenant trackers can opt in for forensics; multi-tenant trackers should leave it off to avoid noise.

polling.description: p0140c: per-tracker polling cadence. Pollers run once per tracker (not per project). N projects sharing one tracker → one polling cycle. Omit when this tracker is webhook-only.

endpoints.description: Jira only: operator-overridable REST endpoint templates. Defaults target Jira Cloud v3; override when Atlassian changes an API path (e.g. CHANGE-2046 removed /rest/api/3/search) so the fix is config, not a redeploy. '{id}' is replaced with the issue key; paths are relative to the tracker url.

project.description: Project entry. References agent/tracker/repos by catalog name. Trigger blocks stay on the project; per-platform trigger settings are project-specific even when two projects share a tracker.

repos.description: List of names from repos: catalog. Always a list, even when there is exactly one. p0140 activates parallel multi-repo execution over this list.

pipeline.description: Deprecated p0140. Single-pipeline shorthand; use pipelines: instead. Still accepted as a one-entry equivalent.

pipelines.description: Pipelines this project hosts. Each entry is either a string (pipeline name, uses project defaults) or an object {name, agent?, skills_path?}. The agent/skills_path overrides are exact-replace lookups, not partial-merge.

resolution.description: p0281b: flat resolution shorthand { strategy-key: value } where key is one of tag|area_path|repo|to_address. Combined with the tracker's type it builds the effective trigger, so a project need not spell out a full per-platform trigger wrapper just to declare routing.

sandbox.description: Per-project sandbox overrides (toolchain image, resources, timeouts, credentials).

images.description: p0245: per-language toolchain image overrides (slug → image), merged over the code-default table per key. Polyglot-safe; the whole-project toolchain_image still wins outright. Keys: dotnet, node, python, go, rust, etc.

secrets.description: p0272: credentials injected into the sandbox pod. Values come from operator-created Kubernetes Secrets — never from this file or context.yaml; only the references are sent to the pod spec.

env.description: Env vars sourced from a k8s Secret. Each value is 'secretName:key', e.g. SF_CLIENT_ID: 'sf-creds:client-id'.

files.description: Secret keys mounted read-only as files (e.g. a JWT key for `sf org login jwt --jwt-key-file`).

webhookTrigger.description: Trigger block shared by GitHub/GitLab/AzureDevOps. pipeline_from_label is optional; when omitted the global pipeline_triggers map applies. project_resolution is REQUIRED (p0140) — declares how the webhook handler picks this project out of all configured projects for an incoming ticket.

oneOf.description: Pipeline name; uses project's default agent and skills_path.

agent.description: Optional override: pipeline runs with this agent instead of the project default. Exact-replace lookup into the agents catalog (no partial-merge).

skills_path.description: Optional override: this pipeline loads skills from a different path than the project default.

projectResolution.description: Tells the webhook handler which agent-smith project owns an incoming ticket. With multi-tenant trackers (one ADO project hosting many sub-projects, one Jira install used by many teams) this declaration is mandatory — without it the handler has no way to route. Strategies: tag (label match on ticket), area_path (ADO AreaPath prefix match — supports both \ and /), repo (GitHub/GitLab source-repo URL match — requires project.repos to have exactly one entry), to_address (Email tracker only, see p0141).

value.description: Strategy-specific match value. tag: label string. area_path: hierarchical path (e.g. ContosoMain/Billing or ContosoMain\Billing); matching is prefix-based on the normalized backslash form, so ContosoMain\Billing matches both itself and ContosoMain\Billing\Invoicing. repo: repo URL or short name matched against the project's single repo's RepoConnection.Url. to_address: full email address.
